perturbation.original_text: 1.10 "Confidential Information" refers to any oral or written materials and information exchanged between the Parties in respect of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the following: 1.10.1 The content of this Agreement and its supplemental agreement(s); 1.10.2 The business (including, but not limited to, business decisions, management methods, operating strategies, incentive strategies, promotional information), operating, financial, technical, product, service information of any party obtained or received by the other party during the performance of this Agreement or during the term of this Agreement; 1.10.3 The other party's user profiles, information, etc.; 1.10.4 The processes and results of settlement of any dispute arising from this Agreement
perturbation.changed_text: 1.10 "Confidential Information" refers to any oral or written materials and information exchanged between the Parties in respect of this Agreement, including, but not limited to, the following: 1.10.1 The content of this Agreement and its supplemental agreement(s); 1.10.2 The business (including, but not limited to, business decisions, management methods, operating strategies, incentive strategies, promotional information), operating, financial, technical, product, service information of any party obtained or received by the other party during the performance of this Agreement or during the term of this Agreement; 1.10.3 Only the other party's user profiles that are publicly available, information, etc.; 1.10.4 The processes and results of settlement of any dispute arising from this Agreement
perturbation.explanation: By limiting 'Confidential Information' to only publicly available user profiles in Section 1.10.3, while the rest of section 1.10 remains untouched, it creates a contradiction with Section 7.1, which broadly protects 'all confidential information' and prohibits disclosure. Now, the definition of confidential information is ambiguious, and the scope of section 7.1 is uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 Each party shall maintain the confidentiality of all confidential information and shall not disclose any confidential information to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party, except for the information which (a) is or will be known by public (not caused by the disclosure to the public by the receiving party); (b) is required to be disclosed by the applicable law or any securities exchange rules or regulations; (c) is necessary to be disclosed to the counsels or financial consultants by either party in respect of the transaction under this Agreement, and such counsels or financial consultants are bound by similar confidentiality obligations hereunder. Disclosure of any confidential information by an employee or agency employed by either party shall be deemed to be a disclosure of such confidential information by that party, and the party shall be liable for breach of this Agreement. This provision shall survive any change or the termination of this Agreement for any reason.
perturbation.justification: The modified text narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' by limiting user profiles to only those 'publicly available' in Section 1.10.3. This directly contradicts Section 7.1, which broadly protects 'all confidential information' without such a limitation. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because Section 7.1 would still be understood to protect all user profiles, while Section 1.10.3 now excludes non-public profiles, creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the scope of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Party B confirms that the deposit paid in accordance with this Agreement is a deposit for the successful cooperation between Party B and Party A on the vehicle operational leasing business and the vehicle financing leasing business to guarantee that Party B will fulfill its obligations under this Agreement and the Cooperation Agreement signed by and between Party A and Party B regarding the vehicle operational leasing business and the Platform Rules and Party B agrees that Party A may deduct reasonable liquidated damages, overdue fines, compensations, etc. from the deposit when Party B breaches this Agreement, or the above mentioned Cooperation Agreement or the Platform Rules. In case that Party A deducts any amount from the deposit, it shall issue a corresponding receipt to Party B.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Party B confirms that the deposit paid in accordance with this Agreement is a deposit for the successful cooperation between Party B and Party A on the vehicle operational leasing business and the vehicle financing leasing business to guarantee that Party B will fulfill its obligations under this Agreement and the Cooperation Agreement signed by and between Party A and Party B regarding the vehicle operational leasing business and the Platform Rules and Party B agrees that Party A may deduct liquidated damages, overdue fines, compensations, etc. from the deposit when Party B breaches this Agreement, or the above mentioned Cooperation Agreement or the Platform Rules. In case that Party A deducts any amount from the deposit, it is not required to issue a corresponding receipt to Party B.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the word 'reasonable', and removing the condition for Party A to issue a receipt after the amount is deducted for 'liquidated damages, overdue fines, compensations, etc.', from the deposit when Party B breaches the agreement, it creates a contradiction between this section 4.1 and section 4.7. As Party A can now deduct any amount without proving to be reasonable, and not issuing a receipt anymore, the act of returning the remaining deposit to party B becomes uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.7 After Party B terminates or rescinds this Agreement, if there is no effective Vehicle Lease Agreement between Party B and a Driver User (if the operational leasing business is conducted) and there is no dispute on the vehicle lease between Party B and a Driver User, Party A shall refund the remaining deposit to Party B on a interest-free basis within 30 days after receipt of the deposit receipt returned by Party B; if there is any effective Vehicle Lease Agreement between Party B and a Driver User or if there is any disputes on the vehicle lease between Party B and a Driver User, Party A shall refund the remaining deposit to Party B on a interest-free basis within 30 days after receipt of the deposit receipt returned by Party B after the driver confirms the Vehicle Lease Agreement has been fulfilled or the dispute has been resolved.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the 'reasonable' qualifier for liquidated damages and eliminates the requirement for Party A to issue a receipt. This directly contradicts section 4.7, which requires a deposit receipt to be returned by Party B before the remaining deposit is refunded. Without a receipt issued by Party A, Party B cannot fulfill the condition in 4.7, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty about the refund process, fulfilling the criteria for Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10.6 In the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement by either party, the breaching party shall remedy such breach within the time limit notified by the observing party. In the event of any losses incurred to the observing party, in addition to the corresponding liability for breach under the Agreement, the breaching party shall also be liable for the compensation for such losses (including, but not limited to, the losses incurred to the counterParty By such breach, the legal costs, notarial fees, appraisal fees, the attorney's fee, the travel expenses, etc., arising from the investigation and affixation of the liabilities of the breaching Party By the counterparty) . Unless any party is in violation of the confidentiality clause, in any case, neither party shall be liable for any indirect, punitive claims, or claims for losses of commercial profits, or damages for business losses of the company or any third Party Arising from this Agreement, or for any loss or inaccuracy of data of any form, whether based on Agreement, tort or any other legal principle, even though the party has been informed of the possibility of such damage.
perturbation.changed_text: 10.6 In the event of any breach of the terms of this Agreement by either party, the breaching party shall remedy such breach within the time limit notified by the observing party. In the event of any losses incurred to the observing party, in addition to the corresponding liability for breach under the Agreement, the breaching party shall also be liable for the compensation for such losses (including, but not limited to, the losses incurred to the counterParty By such breach, the legal costs, notarial fees, appraisal fees, the attorney's fee, the travel expenses, etc., arising from the investigation and affixation of the liabilities of the breaching Party By the counterparty) . In any case, neither party shall be liable for any indirect, punitive claims, or claims for losses of commercial profits, or damages for business losses of the company or any third Party Arising from this Agreement, or for any loss or inaccuracy of data of any form, whether based on Agreement, tort or any other legal principle, even though the party has been informed of the possibility of such damage.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause stating 'Unless any party is in violation of the confidentiality clause', and jumping to the clause 'in any case, neither party shall be liable for any indirect, punitive claims...', creates a contradiction, which states that no party shall be liable for any indirect or punitive claims, not even in a case where the Confidentiality clause is broken. Now Section 10.6 doesn't make it clear what is the remedy for a breach of confidentiality
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.1 Each party shall maintain the confidentiality of all confidential information and shall not disclose any confidential information to any third party without the prior written consent of the other party, except for the information which (a) is or will be known by public (not caused by the disclosure to the public by the receiving party); (b) is required to be disclosed by the applicable law or any securities exchange rules or regulations; (c) is necessary to be disclosed to the counsels or financial consultants by either party in respect of the transaction under this Agreement, and such counsels or financial consultants are bound by similar confidentiality obligations hereunder. Disclosure of any confidential information by an employee or agency employed by either party shall be deemed to be a disclosure of such confidential information by that party, and the party shall be liable for breach of this Agreement. This provision shall survive any change or the termination of this Agreement for any reason.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly carved out an exception for breaches of the confidentiality clause from the limitation of liability for indirect or punitive claims. The changed text removes this exception, creating a direct contradiction with Section 7.1, which outlines the obligations related to confidentiality and states that a party is liable for a breach of this agreement if confidential information is disclosed. By removing the clause 'Unless any party is in violation of the confidentiality clause,' Section 10.6 now contradicts Section 7.1 because even if a party violates the confidentiality clause, Section 10.6 now states that 'In any case, neither party shall be liable for any indirect, punitive claims...', which strongly contradicts the stipulation of liability in Section 7.1.
